data.title: Longitudinal Effect of Electroconvulsive Therapy on Schizophrenia and Bipolar Disorder: a MRI Study
data.brief_summary: For more severe and treatment-resistant cases in schizophrenia and bipolar disorder, electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) is often very effective. The purpose of this study is to investigate the brain structure and function changes after ECT treatment. The neuroimaging marker which may predict the outcome of ECT is also studied in this research.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* International classification of diseases (ICD) diagnosis of Schizophrenia and bipolar disorder;
* Ages 15-70
* All participants are ethnic Han
* Not received ECT treatment before
* PANSS ≥ 60 for schizophrenia subjects
* The patient's written informed consent can be obtained.
endpoints: Changes of Positive And Negative Syndrome Scale(PANSS)for Schizophrenia;
Change of Bech-Rafaelsen Mania Rating Scale (BRMS) for Bipolar disorder
Change of Hamilton Anxiety Scale (HAMA) for Bipolar disorder

data.title: The Effectiveness and Efficacy of the Combination of Pharmacotherapy With the Two New Recovery-oriented Programs, RECOVERYTRSGR (Treatment Resistant Schizophrenia) and RECOVERYTRSBDGR (Treatment Resistant Bipolar Disorder)
data.brief_summary: The effectiveness and efficacy of the combination of pharmacotherapy with the two new recovery-oriented programs, RECOVERYTRSGR for patients with treatment- resistant schizophrenia and RECOVERYTRSBDGR for patients with treatment- resistant bipolar disorder.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria: For patients with treatment resistant schizophrenia:

* age 18-65
* IQ ≥ 80
* Diagnosis TRS.

Inclusion criteria for patients with treatment resistant bipolar disorder:

* age 18-65
* IQ ≥ 80
* Diagnosis TRSBD.

data.title: Medical Phenotyping of NHS General Adult Psychiatry (GAP) Inpatients
data.brief_summary: This observational study will characterise the general psychiatric and general medical phenotypes of 100 adults, sequentially admitted to NHS General Adult Psychiatry (GAP) "mental health" inpatient wards, providing the first detailed information on morbidity in this patient population.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion:

\- Adults aged between 18 and 65 years admitted to GAP wards during recruitment period

Exclusion:

\- Admissions to non-GAP wards (e.g., forensic psychiatry, young people's units, perinatal, old age psychiatry, or general medical wards)

data.title: A Randomised, Controlled Trial to Investigate the Effect of an Intensified Pharmacological Treatment for Schizophrenia, Major Depressive Disorder and Bipolar Depression in Subjects Who Had a First-time Treatment Failure on Their First-line Treatment.
data.brief_summary: Schizophrenia, bipolar and major depressive disorders collectively affect over 10 million people across the EU and are associated with annual healthcare and societal costs in excess of 100 billion Euros. When diagnosed with one of these disorders, patients are prescribed psychotropic medication such as antidepressants, mood stabilisers or antipsychotics. data.title: The Effectiveness and Efficacy of the Combination of Pharmacotherapy and Cognitive Behavioral Psychotherapy Under the Recovery Perspective for Patients With Mental Health Disorders
data.brief_summary: The combination of pharmacotherapy and individual cognitive behavioral therapy in a private practice.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* age 18-65,
* IQ ≥ 80,
* Diagnosis: anxiety disorders, depression, obsessive-compulsive disorders, PTSD, personality disorders, bipolar disorders, schizophrenia and psychotic disorders.

Exclusion Criteria:

\*Substance abuse and head injury.

data.title: A Double Blind Study Examining the Efficacy of Clozapine and a Study of the Pathophysiology in Treatment Resistant Mania
data.brief_summary:
The purpose of this study is to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of clozapine as a treatment for the manic phase of bipolar disorder.

A significant proportion of manic patients either do not respond adequately to conventional treatment or cannot tolerate the adverse effects associated with therapeutic doses of these agents. Clozapine may be a safe and effective treatment for mania. However, the efficacy of clozapine as an alternative therapy in treatment-resistant bipolar disorder mania ha
data.sponsor: National Institutes of Health Clinical Center (CC)
